checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that is either directly extracted from the source PDF or is acceptable AI-generated metadata (such as titles, alt text, and table summaries). No instances of fabricated body text or sections were found.

The source document does not appear to contain specific contact information (phone numbers, emails), and none was added to the output. URLs present in the source are not represented in the output, but no false ones were created.

The output explicitly mentions that '10 sections omitted for token budget'. Several significant sections present in the source PDF are entirely missing from the output, including 'Introduction' (Page 8), 'Statement of Need' (Page 9), 'Existing North Carolina Hydrography Data Status' (Page 9), 'ATLAS Project' (Page 10), and 'Stewardship and Maintenance' (Page 42). This represents a loss of several pages of authoritative content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Introduction (Page 8); Statement of Need (Page 9); ATLAS Project (Page 10); Stewardship and Maintenance (Page 42).
checks.b_evidence_location: Between 'Executive Summary' and 'ATLAS Schema'; after 'Federal Needs'.

checks.a_analysis: Several tables in the output are severely truncated compared to the source. Table 7 (NC Hydro Polyline Attributes, Page 18) has 18 data rows in the source but only 1 in the output. Table 8 (NC Hydro Polygon Attributes, Page 19) has 17 data rows in the source but only 1 in the output. Table 9 (Page 20) and Table 1010 (Page 21) are missing all data rows, showing only headers.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table 7 (Page 18) contains 18 rows (AUID, HUC10AIDX, AUIDA, WB_ID, etc.); Table 8 (Page 19) contains 17 rows; Table 9 (Page 20) contains 9 rows; Table 1010 (Page 21) contains 8 rows.
checks.b_evidence_output: Table 7 (Page 18) in the output shows only 1 row (HYDROID); Table 8 (Page 19) shows 1 row (WaterbodyID); Table 9 and Table 1010 are essentially empty shells with no data rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The sections 'Hydrography Dataset' and 'Water Boundary Dataset' are missing substantial information because the primary data containers (the attribute tables) were truncated or omitted. This results in a loss of more than 25% of the information intended for those specific sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Detailed attribute lists and definitions for line, polygon, and water boundary features (Pages 18-21).
checks.b_evidence_output: Tables containing only one or zero data rows, omitting the bulk of the specification data.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted appears to be accurate. Minor formatting changes (like using checkbox symbols for the summary table) are acceptable and preserve meaning.

checks.a_analysis: I examined the output for repeated text blocks, specifically looking at headings, tables, and paragraphs. The document follows the page flow of the source PDF. The multi-page 'Gap Analysis Summary' table is correctly consolidated into a single structural element in the JSON, and the repeat header from the top of page 46 ('ATLAS Hydrography Gaps Continued') is correctly treated as a label row rather than a duplication of the previous page's content.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows the source document's structure. Major sections are identified appropriately. Subsections within 'NC Hydrography Issue, Gaps, and Recommendations' follow a clean H2-H3-H4 nesting (e.g., Polyline Issues -> Stream Segmentation -> Smoothing). No heading levels are skipped (e.g., H1 to H3).

Significant structural corruption occurred in four consecutive tables (Tables 7, 8, 9, and 10). Tables 7 and 8 contain only the first row of data, omitting nearly twenty rows each. Tables 9 and 10 contain only the header row, omitting all data rows entirely. This represents a major failure in preserving the document's tabular information structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table 7 (Page 18) contains 18 rows of polyline attributes. Table 8 (Page 19) contains 17 rows of polygon attributes. Table 9 (Page 20) contains 9 data rows. Table 10 (Page 21) contains 8 data rows.
checks.b_evidence_output: Table 7 (element in Hydrography Dataset section) only contains 1 data row ('HYDROID'). Table 8 only contains 1 data row ('WaterbodyID'). Table 9 contains 0 data rows. Table 10 contains 0 data rows.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Line Features', 'Polygon Features', 'Recommended Data Schema', and 'Water Boundary Dataset' spanning pages 18-21.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the document accurately reflects the intended flow of the source PDF. Content blocks, including the survey charts (which were intelligently converted into tables for accessibility) and their associated recommendations, follow the linear sequence of the original text.

The document contains a complex nested list on page 30 regarding 'EDH Stream/river collection specifications'. The JSON output successfully captures the nesting levels (main bullet to sub-bullet) and keeps the multi-paragraph sub-bullets together without splitting or incorrectly merging them.

There are no signs of link injection. While the PDF contains several hyperlinked phrases (underlined blue text on page 15), the JSON preserves the text and identifies it as underlined. No URLs were fabricated, and no external links were broken or added without basis.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF is a highly technical document that relies heavily on a integrated visual layout where maps, charts, and diagrams are interspersed with text to provide meaning. In the HTML output, nearly all of these visual elements (with the exception of the cover image and basic tables) are missing. This collapses the complex, visually rich organization of the document into a simple text-and-caption layout. This significantly alters the user's ability to find and interpret the same information in the same way as intended by the source's organization.
checks.b_evidence_source: The PDF features a sophisticated visual flow integrating text with complex technical diagrams (e.g., Table 4 on page 14), maps (e.g., Figure 1 on page 10), and charts (e.g., Figure 11 on page 28).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output presents a simplified single-column text layout where captions exist (e.g., 'Table 4. ATLAS schema...', 'Figure 1. HSSD dataset...', 'Figure 11. Survey Results...') but the corresponding images are entirely absent, leaving only text.

checks.a_analysis: The tables in the document have been successfully converted into HTML table structures. They maintain their columnar integrity, headers are distinguishable, and the content remains readable and understandable. For example, the 'ATLAS_Hydrography_v2 feature table' from PDF page 12 and the multi-page 'Gap Analysis Summary' table at the end of the document (PDF pages 45-46) are rendered correctly in the HTML with clear row and column delineations.

There is a massive failure in image rendering. Dozens of critical, meaningful images—including technical maps of hydrography data, bar charts representing survey results, pie charts, and complex relational data schema diagrams—are missing from the HTML output. While the captions for these figures are present, the images themselves are absent. This includes Figures 1 through 27, and several other unnumbered charts and diagrams.
checks.b_evidence_source: Technical maps and charts throughout PDF pages 9-42, such as Figure 3 (page 21), Figure 11 (page 28), and Figure 27 (page 42).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows captions for these figures but no image data is rendered above them. For example, after the text for Figure 3, the image showing the relationship between HUCs is missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically PDF pages 9, 10, 14, 17, 21-42.

checks.a_analysis: A thorough visual inspection of the HTML output reveals no instances of content duplication. Paragraphs, headings, and tables appear only once in their correct logical sequence.
